Send emails using AWS SES with the Email: Send emails using AWS SES with the EmailService module, including attachments and inline templates with dynamic data.
This service is designed to send emails: This service is designed to send emails using [AWS SES (Simple Email Service)](https://aws.amazon.com/ses/).
`EmailService` is exported from `@mbc-cq: `EmailService` is exported from `@mbc-cqrs-serverless/core` and is registered as a global provider automatically by the framework. Inject it into any service using the standard NestJS constructor injection:
Set the `SES_REGION` and optionally `SES: Set the `SES_REGION` and optionally `SES_FROM_EMAIL` environment variables for SES configuration. See [Environment Variables](/docs/environment-variables) for details.
Composes an email message and immediatel: Composes an email message and immediately queues it for sending. Returns `Promise<SendEmailCommandOutput>` — the AWS SES response includes a `MessageId` string you can capture for tracking:
You can attach files to emails by provid: You can attach files to emails by providing an array of attachment objects:
Sender email address (uses default if no: Sender email address (uses default if not specified)
AWS SES tags for categorization and filt: AWS SES tags for categorization and filtering
Mock `EmailService` in unit tests to avo: Mock `EmailService` in unit tests to avoid making real SES calls:
